_comment_: Phase 2 byte-offset disambiguation anchors for the pinned `.body` of
zawy12/difficulty-algorithms#3 captured in `zawy12_issue_3_lwma1.md`.
Per `docs/design/DAA_LWMA1_PLAN.md` §3 Phase 2 §497-577. Pins LWMA-1's
and LWMA-3's reference-function definitions against upstream reordering
or content insertion that would shift line numbers in the pinned body.
Encoding: the pinned `.body` is UTF-8 with CRLF line endings (the raw
GitHub-API response preserves CRLF). Byte offsets count CRLF bytes.
First-line and last-line fields record the line content verbatim with
line-ending bytes stripped; downstream grep recovery should compare
against rstripped lines from the pinned body. The trailing whitespace
on some upstream lines (LWMA-1 line 77, LWMA-3 line 339) is preserved
in the pinned body but stripped here so the anchor is robust against
editor whitespace normalization.
Range semantics: [byte_offset_start, byte_offset_end) — half-open. The
start anchors at the function-signature line's first byte; the end
anchors immediately after the function's closing brace line (i.e.,
after its trailing CRLF). The LWMA-3 function body contains malformed
C++ (lines 376-381 are a broken/incomplete code snippet preserved
verbatim from upstream); the closing-brace anchor on the column-0 `}`
is therefore a textual delimiter rather than a balanced-brace marker.
DAA_LWMA1.md §5.3 "Issue #3, LWMA-1 reference, lines N–M" citations
resolve against the LWMA-1 byte-offset range, NOT against the full
`.body` line numbers.
